title: Implement Incremental Scanning with File-Watcher
description: Add real-time file-watching and incremental scanning capabilities to the CodeRef scanner. Currently the scanner performs full re-scans on every run, which becomes prohibitively expensive for enterprise monorepos. This workorder implements file-watcher integration (using chokidar) and extends the existing IncrementalCache from the pipeline into the low-level scanner, enabling real-time IDE integration and making the system viable for large codebases.
goal: Enable real-time code indexing that only re-scans changed files, reducing scan time from minutes to milliseconds for large projects and supporting live IDE integration.
requirements: Integrate chokidar file-watcher into scanner architecture
Extend IncrementalCache from pipeline to scanner.ts level
Add optional cache parameter to scanCurrentElements() function
Implement file hash checking before scanning to skip unchanged files
Add --watch flag to CLI for continuous monitoring mode
Handle file creation, modification, and deletion events
Update cache after incremental scans to maintain consistency
Add progress/status output during watch mode
Add fallback to full scan when cache is missing or corrupted
constraints: Must not break existing scan behavior (cache is opt-in)
Must gracefully handle file permission errors
Cache file (.coderef/incremental-cache.json) must remain compatible with pipeline cache

functional: Second scan of unchanged project completes in <100ms (cache hit)
Modifying single file triggers re-scan of only that file
--watch mode continuously monitors and updates index
Cache hit ratio >95% for typical development workflow
performance: Incremental scan 100x faster than full scan on 1000+ file project
Memory overhead <50MB for cache of 10k files
File watcher CPU usage <1% when idle
test_coverage: Performance benchmark comparing full vs incremental scan

out_of_scope: Git integration (using git status for change detection)
